summary: InstantMed's funnel is leaking spend through severe attribution inflation (Google Ads reporting ~100 server conversions vs 18-27 real paid orders) and a recovery system that converts 0 of 119 abandoned intakes. Medical-certificate dominates volume but carries all the abandonment and refund risk. Request-to-checkout captures failed with console errors across all four services, warranting investigation but not yet confirmed as a live user outage.

findings.blocker: Google Ads server conversion count (100) grossly exceeds actual paid orders (Supabase 30d paid=27, local_orders=18). Conversion signal sent to Ads is inflated ~4-5x.
findings.evidence: PostHog google_ads_server_conversion=100 (30d and 90d identical) vs Supabase 30d paid=27, Ads local_orders=18; purchase_completed_server=24 also static across 30d/90d windows
findings.cac_impact: Inflated conversions train Smart Bidding toward non-converting traffic; true CAC ($92.71 local) is masked. Ads CPA $75.85 understates real acquisition cost, driving overspend.
findings.recommended_fix: Audit server-side conversion pipeline; the identical 100/24 counts across 30d and 90d windows indicate a stuck/duplicating or cached export. Reconcile conversion events to Supabase paid orders before trusting any bid optimization.

findings.blocker: Recovery funnel converts 0 of 119 partial intakes despite 6 recovery emails and 21 captured emails. Zero recovered revenue across both 30d and 90d.
findings.evidence: 30d & 90d partial totals: count=119, converted=0, recoverySent=6, withEmail=21; abandonedEmailSent=8 (30d) with converted=0
findings.affected_service: medical-certificate (75 of 119 partials at certificate step)
findings.cac_impact: 119 abandoned intakes at AOV ~$38-40 = ~$4,500 recoverable pipeline fully lost. Recovery sends are firing but converting nobody, wasting the abandonment capture investment.
findings.recommended_fix: Verify recovery email links resume the correct intake/session; only 21 of 119 partials have emails captured, so move email capture earlier (before certificate step). Test the recovery link end-to-end against checkout.

findings.blocker: Massive top-of-funnel drop: 384 intake_started vs 75 checkout_viewed vs 6 client purchase_completed (30d). Only ~20% reach checkout.
findings.evidence: PostHog 30d: intake_started=384, checkout_viewed=75, purchase_completed=6, intake_abandoned_passive=50; certificate partial step count=75
findings.cac_impact: 80% of started intakes never reach checkout. Even small intake-completion gains multiply against $1,668 spend; closing certificate-step drop is the highest-leverage conversion win.
findings.recommended_fix: Instrument per-step drop on the certificate intake (75 partials stall there); review certificate-step form length/validation. Note client purchase_completed (6) vs server (24) gap suggests client event under-fires—verify before trusting client funnel rates.

findings.blocker: Request-to-checkout automation failed for all four services with consoleErrors=1 each, against localhost:3060 base.
findings.evidence: medical-certificate/repeat-prescriptions/erectile-dysfunction/hair-loss request-to-checkout mobile: failed, final=http://localhost:3060/request..., consoleErrors=1, requestFailures=0
findings.cac_impact: If a real checkout JS error exists, every paid click is wasted at the final step. Cannot confirm live outage—captures ran against localhost not production—but blast radius is total if confirmed.
findings.recommended_fix: Re-run request-to-checkout against production (instantmed.com.au) not localhost:3060; capture the console error text. Per evidence-pack rule, scripted completion is not a confirmed outage—triage the console error before drawing conclusions.

findings.blocker: Medical-certificate carries disproportionate refund and abandonment load; paidRate 0.6774 (30d) vs 1.0 for ED/general/repeat-scripts.
findings.evidence: 30d medical-certificate: paidRate=0.6774, refunded=1, refundAud=29.95, abandonedEmailSent=8; other services paidRate=1.0
findings.cac_impact: med-cert is the only ad-attributed service (adAttributed=25 of 26) yet has lowest paid rate, so ad spend funds the weakest-converting funnel. Higher-AOV ED ($49.95) gets zero ad support.
findings.recommended_fix: Consider testing ad budget allocation toward ED/repeat-scripts which convert at 100%; investigate med-cert refund driver (eligibility rejection) to reduce wasted paid intakes.

findings.blocker: Mobile landing pages show elevated request failures (med-cert mobile=3, attribution-continuity mobile=4) vs desktop=1.
findings.evidence: medical-certificate landing mobile requestFailures=3; medical-certificate attribution-continuity mobile requestFailures=4; consoleErrors=0 throughout
findings.cac_impact: device=m traffic dominates ad params; mobile request failures may degrade LCP/conversion on the highest-volume device, inflating CAC for mobile clicks.
findings.recommended_fix: Identify the failing mobile requests (likely analytics/pixel or asset); confirm none are conversion-critical. consoleErrors=0 suggests non-blocking, verify before action.

findings.blocker: Ads spend efficiency degrades over window: 90d local_cac=$119.44 vs 30d local_cac=$92.71, with identical order counts (18) across both windows.
findings.evidence: 90d local_orders=18 local_cac=$119.44 vs 30d local_orders=18 local_cac=$92.71; identical 18 orders implies stale/duplicated local order export
findings.cac_impact: Identical local_orders=18 and local_net=$419.15 across 30d and 90d windows indicates a data freshness/snapshot bug, undermining any CAC trend conclusion.
findings.recommended_fix: Fix the local order export so 30d and 90d windows are independently computed; current identical values make CAC tracking unreliable.

ads_triage: Do NOT scale spend until server conversion inflation (100 vs ~27 real) is reconciled — Smart Bidding is optimizing on bad signal (no live mutation performed).
Recommend capping med-cert spend pending refund/paidRate investigation (0.6774 paid rate absorbs nearly all ad budget).
Recommend testing reallocation toward ED/repeat-scripts (100% paid rate, higher AOV) once checkout capture failures are cleared.
Add negative-keyword review only after re-running production checkout captures; no keyword data quality issues evidenced yet.
measurement_notes: google_ads_server_conversion=100 and purchase_completed_server=24 are IDENTICAL across 30d and 90d windows — strong indicator of a stuck/cached export or duplicated event; treat all conversion counts as suspect.
Supabase local_orders=18 and local_net=$419.15 also identical across 30d/90d — same snapshot/freshness concern.
Client purchase_completed (6) << server purchase_completed (24) and << Supabase paid (27): client-side purchase event under-fires; rely on server/Supabase for true conversion.
intake_funnel_payment_completed (83, 30d) far exceeds purchase_completed and Supabase paid (27) — event naming/definition mismatch; reconcile event taxonomy before trusting funnel rates.
Only 21 of 119 partial intakes have email captured, limiting recovery reach; email capture occurs too late in flow.
Request-to-checkout captures ran against localhost:3060, not production — per evidence-pack rule these are not confirmed user-facing outages and require production re-run.
